title: Negative Prefixes: un-, dis-, in-, im-

explanation.rule: Negative prefixes are added to the beginning of words to create opposite or negative meanings.

groups.note: The most common negative prefix in English

examples.definition: not able to be used or reached
not connected; not in a relationship

groups.note: Creates opposite meaning, especially for verbs and some nouns

examples.definition: to separate or break a connection

groups.note: in- before most consonants; im- before b/m/p; ir- before r; il- before l

examples.definition: not able to happen or be done

wordForms.definition: not able to be used or reached
wordForms.example: The manager was unavailable for comment.
wordForms.rootDefinition: ready or able to be used or obtained

wordForms.example: Some delays are unavoidable during major building works.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to stay away from or not do something

wordForms.definition: to have an unfavourable opinion about something
wordForms.example: Her parents disapprove of her choice of career.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to agree with or accept something officially

wordForms.definition: to break the connection between two things
wordForms.example: Please disconnect the cable before switching off the device.
The discontent among workers led to a strike.
wordForms.rootDefinition: satisfied and happy with a situation

wordForms.example: He seemed incapable of making a decision under pressure.
wordForms.rootDefinition: having the ability or skill to do something

wordForms.definition: not enough or not good enough
wordForms.example: The safety measures were completely inadequate.

wordForms.definition: not relying on others; free from control
wordForms.example: She became financially independent after landing her first job.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to rely on someone or something

wordForms.example: This material is virtually indestructible under normal conditions.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to completely ruin or end something

wordForms.example: His indecisive manner frustrated the rest of the team.
After the accident, he was completely immobile for three weeks.
wordForms.rootDefinition: able to move or be moved

wordForms.example: The journalist claimed the business practices were immoral.
wordForms.rootDefinition: relating to principles of right and wrong

multipleChoiceExercises.sentence: The service was ___ after the technical fault occurred.
Her parents ___ of her decision to drop out of university.
Some noise during construction is, unfortunately, ___.
The new assistant seemed completely ___ of working under pressure.
The referee's decision was seen as completely ___ by the fans.
Growing ___ among staff led the CEO to call a meeting.

transformExercises.sentence: The company's tactics were widely seen as ___. (MORAL)
The roads were ___ during the flooding. (AVAILABLE)
Students who are ___ should ask their teacher for guidance. (DEPEND)
They had to ___ the internet router to fix the problem. (CONNECT)
The funding provided was simply ___ for the scale of the project. (ADEQUATE)
Some risks are ___ in a competitive business environment. (AVOID)
